part.text: I will create the controller file and the two required Twig templates.

input.content:
<?php

namespace App\Controller;

use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;
use Symfony\Component\Routing\Attribute\Route;
use Symfony\Bundle\FrameworkBundle\Controller\AbstractController;

class ArticleController extends AbstractController
{
    #[Route('/articles', name: 'article_index', methods: ['GET'])]
    public function index(): Response
    {
        $articles = [
            ['title' => 'Article Title 1', 'content' => 'Content for the first article.'],
            ['title' => 'Article Title 2', 'content' => 'Content for the second article.'],
            ['title' => 'Article Title 3', 'content' => 'Content for the third article.'],
        ];

        return $this->render('article/index', [
            'articles' => $articles,
        ]);
    }

    #[Route('/articles/{id}', name: 'article_show', methods: ['GET'])]
    public function show(int $id): Response
    {
        // In a real application, this would fetch article data from a repository/database.
        // For now, we hardcode an example article based on $id.
        $article = [
            'id' => $id,
            'title' => 'Article Title ' . $id,
            'content' => 'This is the detailed content for article number ' . $id . '.',
        ];

        return $this->render('article/show', [
            'article' => $article,
        ]);
    }
}
